nodes.goal: Load OSM surface, parks, water, and roads layers for Niterói from Overpass.

nodes.content:
import base64
import requests
import geopandas as gpd
from shapely.geometry import Point

# Fetch the Niterói land surface temperature raster (24 yearly bands,
# 2001–2024) and embed the bytes as base64 inside a single-row
# GeoDataFrame so the downstream join can reuse them without refetching.
url = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/urban-toolkit/autark/main/usecases/public/data/niteroi_lst_verao_2001_2024.tif'
resp = requests.get(url, timeout=120)
resp.raise_for_status()
geotiff_b64 = base64.b64encode(resp.content).decode('ascii')

gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    {'geotiff_b64': [geotiff_b64], 'band_count': [24]},
    geometry=[Point(0, 0)],
    crs='EPSG:4326',
)
return gdf
nodes.goal: Reference the LST GeoTIFF (2001–2024) for Niterói. Body cells carry only the URL; the raster is loaded later in autk-db.
Bundle the OSM layers and the raster URL row into a single 2-element input for the spatial join.
Re-ingest the OSM layers, load the LST raster, then spatially join LST band averages to roads within 1 km.
Per-road OLS regression of yearly LST values via GPU shader.
Render Niterói layers + LST raster with roads thematically colored by warming slope.
Brushable scatterplot of road regression results.

dataflow.name: Autark spatial join + regression
dataflow.description: DuckDB spatial join in JS_COMPUTATION + per-feature OLS GPU regression + linked Autark scatter (Niteroi LST warming, 2001-2024).
